page: Microsoft Azure D8ps_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DPSv5 family, powered by an ARM64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz, with 32.0 GB of system memory. The instance does not include local storage, relying instead on Premium Storage capabilities, and has no integrated GPU.

Performance benchmarks place the D8ps_v5 in the average tier for multi-core operations, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 14501.41 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 5805.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 562.63 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 73.72 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 1030.0 and a poor single-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 7.26 GB/sec. The processor lacks AVX and AMX instruction sets, which limits its utility for advanced vector mathematics and heavy local machine learning tasks, as reflected in weak LLM inference speeds.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based architecture provides dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading, offering predictable compute resource allocation for general-purpose workloads. This makes the server appropriate for workloads such as static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 10.39 GB/sec, Redis database operations, and basic software compilation.
description: Microsoft Azure D8ps_v5 is an ARM64-based general-purpose virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated physical cores on an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor clocked at 3.0 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and integrated GPUs, it relies on remote Premium Storage. Benchmark data indicates average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 14501.41 and a static web serving throughput of 10.39 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is weak, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1030.0 and a poor single-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 7.26 GB/sec. The absence of AVX and AMX instruction sets limits its suitability for vector-heavy calculations. Qualitatively, the dedicated core architecture offers predictable resource density for workloads such as static web serving, Redis operations, and software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8ps_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 32 GB RAM, and Premium Storage capability, offering balanced multi-core performance for web serving and Redis.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8ps_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine with 8 dedicated cores, 32 GB RAM, and average multi-core performance for general workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 virtual machine featuring eight dedicated physical cores and balanced memory allocation for predictable general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 ARM64 physical cores running at 3.0 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
No local storage, utilizing remote Premium Storage capabilities
Average multi-core performance with 10.39 GB/sec static web serving throughput
Weak single-core performance and lack of AVX/AMX vector instructions
Best suited for static web serving, Redis operations, and software compilation
